title: Distributors of technical-grade ethanol for use in the production of hand sanitizers and hard-surface disinfectants - Canada.ca
content:
## Distributors of technical-grade ethanol for use in the production of hand sanitizers and hard-surface disinfectants
Heath Canada introduced an interim measure that permits the use of certain authorized sources of technical-grade ethanol. We took this step to meet the urgent health need for hand sanitizers and disinfectants during the COVID-19 pandemic. We are discontinuing the interim measure as the supply of ethanol has stabilized and the demand for technical-grade ethanol has decreased. Suppliers and distributors are expected to stop producing, reselling and/or distributing technical-grade ethanol. Manufacturers are to return to using approved grades of ethanol for manufacturing after the authorized dates to produce, resell/distribute or sell.
Health Canada granted temporary authorization to the companies listed below to resell and/or distribute approved technical-grade ethanol obtained from an approved supplier to a manufacturer of hand sanitizers and hard-surface disinfectants . To receive temporary authorization, these companies required assessment and authorization by Health Canada.
Technical-grade ethanol obtained from authorized distributors cannot be blended when producing hand sanitizers and hard-surface disinfectants.
The risk assessment summary report contains the information we used to allow and discontinue the temporary use of technical-grade ethanol in hand sanitizers and hard-surface disinfectants.
Companies not on this list may face compliance and enforcement action if they resell and/or distribute technical-grade ethanol for the production of hand sanitizers or hard-surface disinfectants without authorization. You can find information about non-compliant products in the recall advisory .
